title: Reading Time Calculator
description: Count characters and words, then estimate reading and speech time for any text. Supports Japanese and English.

howToUse.label: Paste your text
howToUse.description: Paste the text you want to analyze into the text area.

howToUse.label: Select language and reading speed
howToUse.description: Choose Japanese (default 500 chars/min) or English (default 200 words/min). Adjust the speed to match your personal reading pace.

howToUse.label: Check reading and speech times
howToUse.description: Reading Time is for silent reading; Speech Time is for reading aloud. Useful for adding "X min read" metadata to blog posts.

faq.question: What is the average reading speed in English?
faq.answer: The average adult reads at 200–250 words per minute (wpm). College-level readers often reach 300+ wpm. Speed readers trained in rapid reading techniques can exceed 500 wpm. The default in this tool is 200 wpm for a conservative estimate.

faq.question: How is speech time calculated?
faq.answer: Speech time is estimated at 130 words per minute for English, which reflects a comfortable presentation or narration pace. Actual speaking speed varies by person and context.

faq.question: What reading speed should I use for blog articles?
faq.answer: Medium and most blogging platforms use 200–265 wpm as the standard. The default 200 wpm in this tool gives a slightly conservative estimate — most users will finish faster.

faq.question: How are sentences and paragraphs counted?
faq.answer: Sentences are counted by splitting on terminal punctuation (. ! ? and their Japanese equivalents 。！？). Paragraphs are counted as blocks of text separated by blank lines.

faq.question: Can I change the reading speed?
faq.answer: Yes. Edit the reading speed field to match your personal pace. If you know you read at 300 wpm, enter 300. The calculation updates immediately.
